project.name: @viloforge/vfkb
project.description: ViloForge KnowledgeBase (vfkb) — per-project knowledge memory engine; one engine + two harness faces (Claude Code hooks + Pi extension) + an MCP server over an append-only JSONL brain. Zero runtime deps.

nodes.name: gating.ts
nodes.summary: Guardrail that detects when a tool call would write into the brain directory, used to block agents from corrupting the append-only JSONL store from inside a session.

nodes.name: git.ts
nodes.summary: Thin git wrapper that auto-initializes the brain repo and commits/pushes brain changes, providing durable persistence and cross-clone continuity for the design brain.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps execFileSync for synchronous git invocations rather than spawning async processes.

nodes.name: pi-extension.ts
nodes.summary: The Pi harness face — an in-process TypeScript extension wiring the shared engine into Pi lifecycle hooks (before_agent_start inject, per-turn context, tool-call gating, tool-result capture, session shutdown save).
nodes.languageNotes: Registers async lifecycle handlers against the Pi ExtensionAPI; one of two harness faces over a single engine (ADR-0015).

nodes.name: pi-mcp-bridge.ts
nodes.summary: Bridges the Pi extension to MCP servers, reading MCP client config, connecting via the MCP SDK, and discovering/forwarding available tools.

nodes.name: pi-types.ts
nodes.summary: Hand-written stub type definitions for the Pi extension API and lifecycle events, copied verbatim from the verified mykb spike to avoid silent contract drift.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure TypeScript interface declarations; deliberately mirrors the runtime @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent surface.

nodes.name: secrets.ts
nodes.summary: Secret-detection guardrail that scans text for credential patterns and throws when secrets are found, preventing sensitive data from entering the brain.

nodes.name: session.ts
nodes.summary: Per-session state model tracking injected/captured entry ids, signals, turn counts and resume notes, persisted as JSON session records under the brain directory.

nodes.name: storage.ts
nodes.summary: The append-only JSONL storage kernel — resolves the brain directory, appends and materializes records (tombstone-aware), maintains the context spine and index metadata with content hashing.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements last-write-wins materialization over an append-only log with sha256 content hashing for index freshness.

nodes.name: context.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for context spine and context rendering behavior over a fresh temporary brain.

nodes.name: guardrails.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests covering the guardrail surface — brain-write gating, secret detection, git save, and session state — against isolated temporary brains.

nodes.name: storage.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the JSONL storage kernel — append, materialize, tombstone handling, and meta/index freshness.

nodes.name: isBrainWrite
nodes.summary: Returns whether a given tool name and input would write to a path inside the brain directory, the core check for blocking brain corruption.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Ensures the brain git repo exists, stages changes and commits with a role-attributed message when the working tree is dirty.

nodes.name: saveAndPush
nodes.summary: Commits brain changes via save() and pushes to the remote when one is configured.

nodes.name: resultText
nodes.summary: Reduces a heterogeneous Pi tool result (content array, object, or string) to a bounded text summary suitable for capture.

nodes.name: connect
nodes.summary: Establishes an MCP client connection to a configured server spec via the MCP SDK transport.

nodes.name: discover
nodes.summary: Reads MCP config, connects to each configured server, lists and registers their tools, and wires call-forwarding with error handling and cleanup.

nodes.name: detectSecrets
nodes.summary: Scans text against credential regex patterns and returns the list of secret-kind hits.

nodes.name: assertNoSecrets
nodes.summary: Throws an error listing detected secret kinds if any secrets are present in the given text, used as a write-time guardrail.

nodes.name: SessionState
nodes.summary: Models and persists per-session state — injected/captured entry ids, signals, turn counts, resume notes — to JSON session records, with helpers to load prior records.

nodes.name: brainDir
nodes.summary: Resolves the brain directory from VFKB_DIR or the home-directory default, the path anchor used across the engine.

nodes.name: appendRecord
nodes.summary: Appends a record as a JSONL line to the records file and refreshes index metadata.

nodes.name: readContextSpine
nodes.summary: Reads the trimmed context-spine document from the brain directory if present.

nodes.name: writeContextSpine
nodes.summary: Writes the context-spine document to the brain directory, creating it as needed.

nodes.name: readRecords
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the append-only JSONL records file into an array of record objects.

nodes.name: materialize
nodes.summary: Collapses the append-only record log into current entries via last-write-wins by id, dropping tombstoned entries.

nodes.name: contentHash
nodes.summary: Computes a stable sha256 content hash over materialized entries' id and update timestamps, used to detect index staleness.

nodes.name: readMeta
nodes.summary: Reads the index metadata file describing the last-known content hash and rebuild state.

nodes.name: writeMeta
nodes.summary: Recomputes the content hash from materialized entries and writes refreshed index metadata with a timestamp.

nodes.name: engine.ts
nodes.summary: Central engine facade for the JSONL knowledge brain: write paths (add/update/supersede/transition/capture), decision-family/constitution derivation, injectability + reranking, and all context/resume rendering. The hub that CLI, MCP server, curator, distiller and reader all ground against.
nodes.languageNotes: Functional module with 33 named exports and no classes; combines provenance/trust derivation with append-only record emission to storage.

nodes.name: index-store.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory lexical index over materialized brain records, providing tokenized scored search with freshness invalidation via content-hash meta.
nodes.languageNotes: InMemoryIndex lazily rebuilds when the storage content hash changes; selectIndex is a process-level singleton accessor.

nodes.name: mcp-server.ts
nodes.summary: MCP server entry point exposing the 9 kb_* tools (add/get/list/map/context/search/supersede/transition/resume) over the engine and reader via the @modelcontextprotocol SDK.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses zod-validated tool schemas; main() connects the server over a stdio transport.

nodes.name: read.ts
nodes.summary: Query/read layer that filters, term-ratio-floors, and reranks indexed entries by type/zone/role/tags/status, including the verified trust filter; exposes query and queryExplained.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript type definitions for the knowledge brain — entry types, zones, roles, provenance/validity shapes and the Entry record contract used across the engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type/interface declarations with no runtime exports; imported widely for structural typing.

nodes.name: curator.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the curator promotion/corroboration flow, exercising engine writes and read queries against a fresh temp brain.

nodes.name: decision-family.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests covering decision-family behavior in the engine: ordinal stamping, supersession, transitions, and constitution derivation.

nodes.name: engine.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for core engine operations — entry creation, trust derivation, context map building, and rendering against a temp brain.

nodes.name: read.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the read/query layer covering filtering, term-ratio flooring, reranking, and the verified trust filter.

nodes.name: deriveTrust
nodes.summary: Maps an author role to a trust tier used for ranking and trust glyphs.

nodes.name: addEntry
nodes.summary: Creates a new knowledge entry: scrubs for secrets, assigns id/timestamps/trust, marks decision-family records, and appends an immutable record to storage.

nodes.name: updateEntry
nodes.summary: Applies a patch to an existing fluid-type entry by appending an updated record with a refreshed timestamp.

nodes.name: setProvenanceStatus
nodes.summary: Sets the provenance trust status of an entry (e.g. verified) by appending an updated record.

nodes.name: buildContextMap
nodes.summary: Aggregates live entries into a context map of counts by type, top tags, and decision-family stats.

nodes.name: renderContextMap
nodes.summary: Renders a context map into a compact human-readable summary string.

nodes.name: supersede
nodes.summary: Supersedes an existing entry with new text, linking the new record back to the old one for decision-family lineage.

nodes.name: transitionDecision
nodes.summary: Transitions a decision-family entry to a new status (e.g. accepted/superseded) by appending an updated record.

nodes.name: supersededIds
nodes.summary: Collects the set of entry ids that have been superseded by later records.

nodes.name: effectiveStatus
nodes.summary: Computes an entry's effective status, accounting for supersession.

nodes.name: stampOrdinals
nodes.summary: Assigns stable monotonic ADR ordinals to decision-family entries based on first-seen order.

nodes.name: deriveConstitution
nodes.summary: Derives the ordered set of accepted constitutional decisions from live decision-family entries.

nodes.name: isInjectable
nodes.summary: Determines whether an entry is eligible for context injection given supersession and validity-window constraints.

nodes.name: heuristicCompare
nodes.summary: Comparator ranking entries by within-tier trust score then recency.

nodes.name: rerank
nodes.summary: Reranks a list of entries using the heuristic trust/recency comparator.

nodes.name: renderContextBundle
nodes.summary: Builds the injectable context bundle (constitution + reranked entries) within a character budget for session injection.

nodes.name: initContextSpine
nodes.summary: Initializes the context spine file if absent so renderContext has a stable backbone.

nodes.name: renderContext
nodes.summary: Renders the full project context document combining the context spine, constitution, decision list, context map, and injectable entries.

nodes.name: renderNaiveDump
nodes.summary: Renders a budget-limited chronological dump of non-archived entries as a baseline/comparison view.

nodes.name: classifyToolOutcome
nodes.summary: Classifies a captured tool-call result as success or failure from stderr/output heuristics.

nodes.name: captureToolCall
nodes.summary: Captures a non-knowledge tool invocation into the brain as an entry, recording inputs and classified outcome.

nodes.name: currentInjectableIds
nodes.summary: Returns the ids of all entries currently eligible for context injection.

nodes.name: renderContextDelta
nodes.summary: Computes the per-turn delta of freshly injectable entries not yet injected in the session, marking them as injected.

nodes.name: renderResumeDigest
nodes.summary: Builds the session resume digest from the last session window: distilled lessons, signal tallies, and recent entries.

nodes.name: renderResume
nodes.summary: Top-level resume renderer combining the session digest with the injectable context bundle for session handoff.

nodes.name: InMemoryIndex
nodes.summary: Lexical in-memory index that rebuilds on content-hash change and performs tokenized scored search over brain entries.

nodes.name: selectIndex
nodes.summary: Returns the process-level singleton InMemoryIndex instance.

nodes.name: queryTermCount
nodes.summary: Counts the distinct tokenized terms in a query string for term-ratio scoring.

nodes.name: renderNoMatch
nodes.summary: Renders a helpful no-match response for kb_search, surfacing diagnostics like filtered-out counts.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core query engine: scores via the lexical index, applies the term-ratio floor and type/zone/role/tag/status/injectability filters, then reranks and slices results with optional explain diagnostics.

nodes.name: cli.ts
nodes.summary: The Claude Code harness face: a CLI entry point whose main() dispatches every subcommand (add/list/search/query/map/context/resume/resume-note/curate/distill/save/supersede) and the session-start, pre-tool-use and post-tool-use hooks into the engine, curator, distiller, gating and git layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large async main() with a string-switch command router and stdin-driven hook handlers; uses small helper parsers (flag/cleanText/isFlagValue) for argv.

nodes.name: readStdin
nodes.summary: Reads piped stdin into a string with a 2s unref'd timeout fallback, used to feed JSON hook payloads to the pre/post-tool-use handlers.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Core command router that parses argv and dispatches each subcommand and Claude Code hook to engine reads/writes, the curator, the distiller, gating checks and git save.

nodes.name: cleanText
nodes.summary: Reassembles free-text command arguments while stripping flag tokens and their values into a clean entry body string.

nodes.name: counters.ts
nodes.summary: Append-only signal ledger that records corroboration signals (per entry id/kind/source) under the brain's .signals directory and tallies them for the curator's promotion logic.

nodes.name: recordSignal
nodes.summary: Appends a timestamped corroboration signal record (entry id, kind, source) as a JSONL line under the brain's signals file.

nodes.name: readSignals
nodes.summary: Reads and parses all recorded signal records from the signals JSONL file, returning an empty list when none exist.

nodes.name: tally
nodes.summary: Aggregates the recorded signals for a single entry id into a count keyed by signal kind/source for promotion eligibility checks.

nodes.name: tallies
nodes.summary: Builds a per-entry-id map of aggregated signal tallies across all recorded signals.

nodes.name: curator.ts
nodes.summary: The ACE curator: promotes corroborated entries to verified trust, archives or supersedes decisions, merges lexical duplicates, and detects lexical duplicate candidates against the engine and signal counters.

nodes.name: promote
nodes.summary: Promotes a non-decision entry by updating its trust/provenance via the engine's updateEntry.

nodes.name: eligibleForPromotion
nodes.summary: Returns whether an entry's signal tally meets the promotion threshold.

nodes.name: promoteIfCorroborated
nodes.summary: Promotes an entry to verified status when its corroboration signal tally crosses the threshold, also stamping provenance via the engine.

nodes.name: archive
nodes.summary: Archives an entry, transitioning decision-family entries via the engine's transitionDecision and others via updateEntry.

nodes.name: mergeDuplicate
nodes.summary: Merges a losing duplicate entry into a winner, updating the loser's status through the engine.

nodes.name: findLexicalDuplicates
nodes.summary: Scans all entries and reports lexically near-identical non-decision pairs by normalized text for duplicate review.

nodes.name: distiller.ts
nodes.summary: The auto-distiller: clusters captured error tool-calls by a normalized error signature, proposes gotcha candidates, and on repeated corroboration creates or reinforces distilled gotcha entries via the engine and signal counters.

nodes.name: errorCaptures
nodes.summary: Filters all entries down to error-classified tool-call captures (excluding vfkb's own tooling) for distillation.

nodes.name: distillCandidates
nodes.summary: Groups error captures by normalized error signature into candidate gotcha clusters with their source entry ids.

nodes.name: distill
nodes.summary: Runs distillation: for each candidate cluster either records a corroboration signal against an existing distilled gotcha or creates a new gotcha entry via the engine.

nodes.name: distiller.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite exercising the distiller end-to-end against a fresh temp brain: captures error tool-calls and asserts candidate clustering, gotcha creation, and corroboration behavior.

nodes.name: resume.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite covering session resume/digest rendering against a fresh temp brain, validating engine resume output, session state, and curator/counter interactions.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Phase-0 deployment proof container: drops the prebuilt dist/ artifact into a clean node:20-slim image with no npm install or native build, proving vfkb's zero-runtime-dep faces load and run unaided.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build that deliberately omits node_modules and any install step to validate that vfkb has no runtime dependencies; ENTRYPOINT ["bash", "-lc"] lets the harness pass arbitrary commands at run time.

nodes.name: node
nodes.summary: node:20-slim runtime stage that sets WORKDIR /vfkb, copies in the built dist/ and package.json, and configures VFKB_DIR=/brain for the brain directory.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage node:20-slim image for the devops-kb spike: a clean-room Claude Code / pi agent session layered with real Azure infra tooling (az, kubectl, glab, terraform via tfenv, ansible) plus the built vfkb knowledge substrate and a seeded devops-kb brain.
nodes.languageNotes: Each toolchain is installed in its own RUN layer (Azure CLI, kubectl, glab, tfenv, ansible venv) for cacheability; ansible uses a PEP-668-safe venv and image-created dirs are chmod 0777 to avoid uid-mismatch write failures when run with --user.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion list, keeping node_modules, build artifacts, and local state out of the L4 evaluation container images.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code MCP client configuration that registers the local vfkb MCP server (dist/mcp-server.js) exposing the 9 knowledge-base tools.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide and continuity protocol for Claude Code working in the vfkb repo, covering the dogfooded .vfkb design brain, build/test/run, the ASDLC process, the L4 harness, commit rules, and key source files.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview describing vfkb's purpose, layout, build/test workflow, the pi MCP bridge, and the dockerized L4 scenario harness for proving the knowledge engine's value.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM package manifest for @viloforge/vfkb declaring the build/test scripts, the vfkb and vfkb-mcp bin entries, ESM exports, and the minimal runtime dependencies (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod).

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration emitting the dist/ build from src/, tuned for the stdlib-only Node engine and MCP server.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Core design document for vfkb that locks the architectural decisions (D1–D7) — scope boundary, structural/topology model, entry schema, write/concurrency mechanics, read/query interface, runtime/deployment, and automatic context-injection with passive capture.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured as a sequence of LOCKED decisions (D1–D7) mirroring the engine's module boundaries.

nodes.name: FEATURES.md
nodes.summary: Feature narrative describing vfkb as the shared memory for an AI software factory, covering git-native storage, causal consistency, multi-agent attribution, concurrency, the trust gradient, the project context doc, and the MCP query surface.

nodes.name: H2a-fleet-wiring-PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for wiring vfkb into the live agent fleet via a stdio-local MCP server (not HTTP-served), enumerating changes to vafi and vfkb, the verification gate, sequencing, and risks.

nodes.name: H4-DEVELOPMENT-ROADMAP.md
nodes.summary: The active in-repo execution authority: a sequenced, dependency-ordered roadmap covering session continuity/auto-distill, search robustness, cross-harness parity, dockerized L4 methodology, and L4 scenario coverage, with a ratified order and execution protocol.
nodes.languageNotes: Tracks 1–5 are dependency-ordered with explicit gate states; §4 is the binding execution order.

nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md
nodes.summary: v1 (per-project tier) implementation plan: build discipline, lessons from the mykb spike, locked decisions (ADR-0011…0015), the TypeScript engine-plus-faces architecture, the phased TDD build, testing strategy, and deferred items.

nodes.name: STATUS-AND-ROADMAP.md
nodes.summary: North-star status-and-roadmap doc capturing where vfkb sits today, the shipped per-project feature surface and L4 acceptance, what v1 deliberately deferred, and the risk-sequenced H0–H4 roadmap.

nodes.name: ADR-0001-record-decisions-as-adrs.md
nodes.summary: Decides that all significant vfkb architecture decisions are recorded as immutable Nygard-format ADRs; a change of mind is a new superseding ADR rather than an in-place edit, preserving the rationale and evolution of thinking.

nodes.name: ADR-0002-greenfield-reimplementation.md
nodes.summary: Decides to build vfkb greenfield in TypeScript, treating mykb as a reference/oracle only (zero code inheritance) while harvesting its lessons, to avoid inheriting single-user assumptions.

nodes.name: ADR-0003-language-typescript.md
nodes.summary: Selects TypeScript as the implementation language so the in-process Pi auto-injection extension and the engine share one codebase; supersedes the prior Python position and rejects Go for the same in-process reason.

nodes.name: ADR-0004-decision-is-adr-grade.md
nodes.summary: Elevates the `decision` entry type to ADR-grade with a six-field template, a status lifecycle, and immutable-supersede semantics for the decision family while keeping fact/gotcha/pattern/link editable.

nodes.name: ADR-0005-injection-filters-stale.md
nodes.summary: Defines the auto-injection (push) filter: it excludes superseded/deprecated/archived entries from the live window but includes trust-labelled unverified entries; pull queries may still return stale entries on request.

nodes.name: ADR-0006-context-map.md
nodes.summary: Adopts a derived-first Context Map (v1 = Index/Topology layer only) auto-injected at session start to counter hallucination-by-omission, deferring the Glossary and Routing Table layers to the global tier.

nodes.name: ADR-0007-rfc-is-proposed-decision.md
nodes.summary: Models an RFC as a `decision` in `proposed` status with an open comment period (comments as related entries) rather than introducing a new entry type, leveraging ADR-0004's status lifecycle.

nodes.name: ADR-0008-constitution-tier.md
nodes.summary: Models constitutional rules as `constitutional`-flagged decisions plus a derived, always-injected Agent Constitution document; establishes the Driver (Constitution) vs Brakes (Context Gates) vocabulary and retires "guardrails."

nodes.name: ADR-0009-decision-identity-and-numbering.md
nodes.summary: Splits identity from display: a nanoid is the canonical collision-free identity used by all references, while the human-facing ADR ordinal is assigned by the engine when a decision lands on serialized `main`.

nodes.name: ADR-0010-product-vision.md
nodes.summary: Models the Product Vision tier with existing primitives — narrative taste in the context doc plus editable `pattern` heuristics tagged vision/heuristic — completing the Constitution → Vision → Specs hierarchy without a new type or VISION.md.

nodes.name: ADR-0011-envelope-richness.md
nodes.summary: Adopts a bi-temporal validity window and structured provenance origin into the schema-v1 entry envelope, derives trust rather than storing it, and phases consumption to pre-empt mykb's #1 retrofit debt.

nodes.name: ADR-0012-two-stage-retrieval.md
nodes.summary: Adopts a pluggable two-stage retrieval pipeline right-sized for the flat per-project tier, shipping the pure-local Heuristic reranker as the v1 default with Stage-1 candidate-narrowing as pass-through and the EmbeddingReranker stubbed; later amended by ADR-0016.

nodes.name: ADR-0013-no-hard-native-dep.md
nodes.summary: Requires zero hard native dependency: a pluggable Index with a pure-JS in-memory default and an optional auto-detected SQLite/FTS5 backend that degrades gracefully, killing mykb's native-compile deployment pain across agent images.

nodes.name: ADR-0014-index-freshness.md
nodes.summary: Defines index freshness as content-derived (a content_hash token) plus explicit rebuild, never mtime — surviving git mtime rewrites — and makes synchronous index regen a guaranteed side-effect of every brain-mutating write.

nodes.name: ADR-0015-cross-harness-auto-layer.md
nodes.summary: Adopts a tiered cross-harness auto-layer with full parity for session-start injection and passive capture but Pi-only per-turn push (Claude Code's UserPromptSubmit is unreliable, degrading to an MCP-pull floor), budgeting the session-start bundle to Claude Code's 10k-char cap.

nodes.name: ADR-0016-search-ranking-and-embedding-revisit.md
nodes.summary: Amends ADR-0012 after a live dogfood failure: explicit text search becomes relevance-primary (vs the injection Heuristic order), adds light lexical stemming, and promotes the EmbeddingReranker from deferred to Planned-pending-verification behind a gate; establishes the "Amended by" status marker.

nodes.name: ADR-0017-search-relevance-floor.md
nodes.summary: Amends ADR-0016 by adding a relative relevance floor to explicit search based on distinct query-term coverage (default minTermRatio 1/3), dropping incidental single-term weak matches that masquerade as answers; explicit-search only, injection unchanged.

nodes.name: ADR-0018-honest-no-match-contract.md
nodes.summary: Amends ADR-0016 and builds on ADR-0017 with a cause-distinguished honest no-match contract: queryExplained returns a deterministic diagnosis (empty_topic / no_match / all_filtered) with a filter-reason tally and a labelled below-floor hint, so agents cannot launder model priors as recorded knowledge.

nodes.name: ADR-0019-self-hosted-design-brain.md
nodes.summary: Decides vfkb self-hosts its own per-project brain by committing .vfkb/entries.jsonl (with a link entry per ADR/RFC rather than copied decisions) and native fact/gotcha/pattern knowledge, dogfooding ADR-0001/0004 while keeping the index rebuildable per ADR-0014.

nodes.name: ADR-0020-session-continuity-record.md
nodes.summary: Decides vfkb builds its knowledge half of session continuity as a derived, append-only per-session record (not a work-state tracker), rendering a Tier-A resume injection and kb_resume, structurally defeating the forgotten/stale/clobbered handoff failures.

nodes.name: ADR-0021-auto-distill-and-curator.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision establishing auto-distill on capture (raw tool calls distil into the `incoming` zone) and an ACE-style curator that promotes via deltas and counters rather than rewriting entries. Later amended by ADR-0024.

nodes.name: ADR-0022-l4-evaluation-methodology.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision defining the L4 evaluation methodology as dockerized, reproducible, contrast-based, multi-trial, and dual-harness (pi + claude) with self-contained images. Establishes the live purpose-demonstration substrate.

nodes.name: ADR-0023-scenario-contract-first.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision that agent-observable features are scenario-contract-first: the L4 scenario is the Definition of Done, run RED before implementation, while structural invariants remain deterministic unit tests.

nodes.name: ADR-0024-relabel-trust-on-promotion.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision that corroborated promotion re-stamps provenance to `verified` so trust elevation is agent-observable, and the distiller stops baking '(unverified)' into entry text. Amends ADR-0021.

nodes.name: ADR-0025-project-context-doc-and-kb-context.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision introducing the project context doc plus the `kb_context` tool — an assembled 'agent's first read' combining an authored spine with derived Constitution, Context Map, and decisions, read on demand.

nodes.name: ADR-0026-rebrand-to-vfkb.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision recording the full hard rename vtfkb → vfkb (ViloForge KnowledgeBase) across identity, env vars, dirs, and repo, including an operator-authorised branding-only sweep of the immutable ADRs 0001–0025.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index and authoritative register of vfkb's Architecture Decision Records, documenting the Nygard format rules, immutability lifecycle (per ADR-0001), and a table of all ADRs 0001–0026 with their status.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index for vfkb's RFCs, explaining that an RFC is a proposed decision (ADR-0007) in Nygard format that becomes an ADR with an ordinal stamped at merge, with a status table mapping RFC-001..007 to their accepted ADRs.

nodes.name: RFC-001-search-relevance-floor.md
nodes.summary: Proposes that explicit search apply a relative relevance floor so small result limits don't surface the best of the noise; refines ADR-0016's relevance-primary search. Accepted as ADR-0017.

nodes.name: RFC-002-honest-no-match-contract.md
nodes.summary: Proposes that search report an explicit, cause-distinguished no-match (e.g. all_filtered vs empty) instead of a bare empty array. Accepted as ADR-0018.

nodes.name: RFC-003-embedding-accuracy-mode.md
nodes.summary: Proposes shipping the EmbeddingReranker as an opt-in 'accuracy mode' search tier reserved by ADR-0012, honoring the no-hard-native-dep constraint (ADR-0013). Status: Proposed (gated, not yet an ADR).

nodes.name: RFC-004-self-hosted-design-brain.md
nodes.summary: Proposes that vfkb self-host its own design-brain by committing .vfkb/ with ADRs linked not copied, dogfooding the per-project tier on its own repo. Accepted as ADR-0019.

nodes.name: RFC-005-session-continuity-record.md
nodes.summary: Proposes session continuity as a derived, append-only knowledge-continuity record (vfkb's half of the vtf/vfkb seam), replacing a hand-written handoff slot. Accepted as ADR-0020.

nodes.name: RFC-006-auto-distill-and-curator.md
nodes.summary: Proposes auto-distill (capture distils to the incoming zone) plus an ACE curator that maintains the brain via deltas and counters rather than rewrites, with explicit safety rails. Accepted as ADR-0021.

nodes.name: RFC-007-project-context-doc-and-kb-context.md
nodes.summary: Proposes a project context doc plus a kb_context capability that assembles an 'agent's first read' from an authored narrative spine and derived sections. Accepted as ADR-0025.

nodes.name: claude-code__claude-haiku-4-5.json
nodes.summary: L4 behavior record (raw JSON) for the claude harness on claude-code/claude-haiku-4-5; 22 scenarios recorded, 21 demonstrated. Non-dockerized run at vfkb ebc6daf.

nodes.name: claude-code__claude-haiku-4-5.md
nodes.summary: Human-readable scoreboard for the claude harness on claude-code/claude-haiku-4-5 L4 run (22 scenarios, 21 demonstrated); only tool-gating failed to hold.

nodes.name: claude-code__claude-haiku-4-5__docker.json

nodes.name: PHASE0-REPORT.md
nodes.summary: Phase 0 spike report documenting the cross-harness auto-layer de-risking: proves the engine -> harness -> model injection path works live on both Claude Code and Pi, with tool-call capture, 10k budget fit, zero-native-dep container deployment, and prompt-cache participation (ADR-0011..0015).
nodes.languageNotes: Results table maps each de-risking gate to its method and observed evidence; emphasizes external-effect proof (BANANA-42 canary) over agent self-report.

nodes.name: container-smoke.sh
nodes.summary: In-container smoke test proving the zero-install deployment path: loads the engine/CLI, writes facts, renders the Tier-A context block (<=10k), validates the SessionStart hook JSON contract, loads the Pi extension module, and verifies Tier-B tool-call capture.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail and inline node -e assertions to check ground truth (e.g. grep DOCKER-OK) rather than trusting self-report.

nodes.name: dogfood-smoke.sh
nodes.summary: Stage-1 headless acceptance smoke for the vfkb container dogfood: seeds a distinctive fact, asserts Tier-A SessionStart injection, captures a real Claude session's tool call (Tier-B), checks round-trip surfacing, mykb isolation, MCP face usability, and the RFC-001/RFC-002 relevance-floor and honest no-match behaviors.
nodes.languageNotes: Every assertion checks ground truth (brain file / hook output) against a throwaway brain; uses bounded retry on the LLM MCP round-trip to tolerate the cold-start race while keeping a deterministic tools/list preflight as the real gate.

nodes.name: mcp-config.json
nodes.summary: MCP server configuration registering the vfkb MCP server (node dist/mcp-server.js) with an isolated VFKB_DIR, used by the spike to wire the MCP face into a Claude session.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code settings wiring the vfkb hooks for the Phase 0 spike: SessionStart (inject context bundle), PreToolUse on edit tools, and PostToolUse on all tools (capture), each invoking dist/cli.js with an isolated VFKB_DIR.

nodes.name: vtfkb-dogfood.sh
nodes.summary: Launches the clean-room vfkb dogfood: builds the image, synthesizes a minimal ~/.claude.json (oauth identity only), and runs an interactive Claude Code session inside the deployment-shaped container with both vfkb faces (MCP + session hooks) wired against a persistent brain and the live repo.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses --strict-mcp-config plus a synthetic claude.json to guarantee isolation from mykb/global config; exec's docker run with credential and config mounts.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: System-prompt brief for the devops-kb demo: positions Claude as an Azure infrastructure operator wired to a seeded vfkb knowledge store, with recall-before-act guidance and non-negotiable read-only/mutation safety rules.

nodes.name: devops-kb-pi.sh
nodes.summary: Launcher that runs the clean-room, tooled Azure-ops session through the pi harness on GitHub Copilot (Claude Haiku 4.5), wiring vfkb as pi extensions plus an infra-guard mutation gate inside a Docker container with mounted brain, Azure, and SSH credentials.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash with strict mode (set -euo pipefail); builds a synthetic clean-room ~/.pi/agent and pins an ed25519 SSH key to work around modern GitLab/OpenSSH SHA-1 rejection.

nodes.name: devops-kb.sh
nodes.summary: Launcher for the clean-room, tooled Claude Code session for Azure infra ops, carrying the seeded devops-kb brain and enforcing mutation prompting via the bash-guard PreToolUse hook in a Docker container.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash strict mode; synthesizes a minimal ~/.claude.json (account identity only) to isolate from the host mykb projects/MCP/history.

nodes.name: mcp-config.json
nodes.summary: MCP server configuration registering the vfkb knowledge engine (node mcp-server.js) for the devops-kb session, pointing VFKB_DIR at the mounted /brain and project devops-kb.

nodes.name: operating-rules.md
nodes.summary: Operating-rules document appended as the pi system prompt for the devops-kb session, mirroring CLAUDE.md's knowledge-store recall/capture guidance, tooling list, and non-negotiable infrastructure safety rules.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code settings for the devops-kb session: wires vfkb session-start/pre/post-tool-use hooks, a bash-guard PreToolUse mutation gate, and an allow-list of read/edit and mcp__vfkb permissions.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Baked working-context guide for the clean-room dogfood container, instructing the agent to recall/capture knowledge via the vfkb MCP tools and orienting it to the repo layout.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the containerized clean-room Claude Code dogfood session that isolates vfkb from host context, covering the container rationale, auth model, usage, and the supporting files.

nodes.name: mcp-config.json
nodes.summary: MCP server configuration wiring the vfkb MCP face (dist/mcp-server.js) into the dogfood container with VFKB_DIR pointed at the persistent /brain.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code hooks configuration registering vfkb SessionStart inject plus Pre/PostToolUse passive capture and brain-write gate, all routed through dist/cli.js with VFKB_DIR=/brain.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code harness settings wiring the vfkb CLI hooks (session-start / pre- and post-tool-use) so editor sessions capture knowledge into the brain.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern list controlling which paths the Understand-Anything analyzer skips when scanning this repository.

nodes.name: entries.jsonl
nodes.summary: The committed append-only JSONL design-brain for vfkb — the durable knowledge entries (decisions, facts, gotchas) that ship with the repo and seed cross-clone continuity.
nodes.languageNotes: JSONL append-only log: one self-contained JSON entry per line, the storage substrate of the engine.

nodes.name: compare.mjs
nodes.summary: Cross-model L4 report-card tool that loads the per-model behavior records produced by l4-purpose.mjs and renders a side-by-side comparison table.

nodes.name: build.sh
nodes.summary: Shell helper that builds the dockerized L4 harness images (pi and claude faces) used by the reproducible evaluation runner.

nodes.name: claude.Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker image definition for the Claude-Code face of the L4 evaluation harness, baking the built vfkb engine and MCP server for sandboxed agent runs.

nodes.name: models.json
nodes.summary: Provider/model registry consumed by the L4 harness, declaring the LLM providers and model ids available to dockerized evaluation runs.

nodes.name: pi.Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker image definition for the Pi face of the L4 evaluation harness, packaging the vfkb Pi extension and MCP server for sandboxed agent runs.

nodes.name: l4-purpose.mjs
nodes.summary: The L4 purpose-demonstration harness driver: a dockerized, dual-harness (pi + claude), N=3 runner that seeds throwaway brains, drives real agents against scenarios, and records whether the knowledge brain measurably changed agent behavior.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained ESM driver spawning node/claude/pi/docker via execFileSync; each scenario builds isolated temp brains and asserts on captured output.

nodes.name: claudeCredsDir
nodes.summary: Resolves (or materializes into a temp dir) the Claude credentials directory so dockerized claude-face runs can authenticate via the Max-subscription OAuth.

nodes.name: claudeSettings
nodes.summary: Generates the per-run Claude Code settings.json (hook wiring and capabilities) for a given throwaway brain and writes it into the run's temp workspace.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Core dispatcher that drives a single agent turn against a brain across either harness (claude or pi), host or docker, assembling CLI/MCP args and returning the agent's output.

nodes.name: qa
nodes.summary: Scenario assertion/registration helper that runs a named check N times and records the demonstrated/failed verdict per the ≥2/3-trials rule.

nodes.name: seed-self-brain.mjs
nodes.summary: One-shot seeding script that populates vfkb's self-hosted design brain (.vfkb) with foundational entries derived from the repo's ADRs and docs.

nodes.name: bash-guard.mjs
nodes.summary: Spike guard hook that inspects bash commands and emits an allow/deny decision as JSON, exploring devops command gating against a knowledge base.

nodes.name: gitconfig
nodes.summary: Spike git configuration snippet wiring the devops-kb guard hooks into git operations.

nodes.name: infra-guard.mjs
nodes.summary: Spike infrastructure guard that classifies commands as destructive/binary/read-only to prototype knowledge-driven infra-change gating.

nodes.name: classify
nodes.summary: Classifies an infra command string against destructive/binary/read-only pattern sets, returning the matched risk category.

nodes.name: migrate-seed.mjs
nodes.summary: Spike migration/seeding script that loads devops gotcha knowledge into a brain to prototype the devops-kb workflow.

nodes.name: dogfood.Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Docker image used for dogfood smoke-testing the vfkb engine and MCP server in an isolated container.

nodes.name: mcp-preflight.mjs
nodes.summary: Deterministic, LLM-free preflight that boots the vfkb MCP server over stdio and asserts it advertises its full required tool surface (initialize + tools/list).

nodes.name: hook.test.ts
nodes.summary: End-to-end test exercising the built CLI hook (post-tool-use capture face) as a child process and asserting captured entries land in the brain.

nodes.name: mcp.test.ts
nodes.summary: Protocol-level e2e test where a real MCP SDK client speaks to the compiled vfkb MCP server over stdio, exercising the initialize handshake, tools/list and tools/call wire protocol.

nodes.name: isDecisionFamily
nodes.summary: Returns whether an entry type belongs to the immutable decision family (decision/RFC/constitutional), gating supersede-vs-edit semantics across the engine.

nodes.name: readAll
nodes.summary: Returns all current knowledge entries by materializing the append-only log; the primary read primitive the rest of the engine builds on.

nodes.name: queryExplained
nodes.summary: Public query entry point that delegates to run() returning a SearchResult with explain diagnostics for callers that need ranking/filter introspection.

layers.name: Storage Kernel
layers.description: The append-only JSONL persistence kernel and shared primitives — storage spine, in-memory index, type definitions, counters, secret redaction, and the git wrapper for durable cross-clone brain persistence.

layers.name: Engine & Knowledge Logic
layers.description: The single shared engine layer: filtering/reranking/rendering, the query/trust-filter read path, the ACE curator and distiller, session continuity, and the brain-write gating guardrail.

layers.name: Harness Faces
layers.description: The two harness faces and MCP face over one engine — Claude Code hooks (cli.ts), the Pi in-process extension plus its MCP bridge and types, and the standalone MCP server exposing the 9 kb_* tools.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Vitest unit and integration suites covering engine, storage, decision-family, context, hooks, MCP, read, curator, distiller, resume, and guardrail invariants.

layers.name: Documentation & Decisions
layers.description: The ASDLC decision record — DESIGN/FEATURES/roadmaps plus the immutable ADR (0001-0026) and RFC (001-007) corpus that governs how the project is built.

layers.name: L4 Evaluation Harness
layers.description: The dockerized, reproducible L4 purpose-demonstration eval driver, comparison tooling, container build definitions, and the recorded dual-harness trial results.

layers.name: Phase-0 Spike & Prototypes
layers.description: The Phase-0 spike workspace — report, smoke/dogfood scripts, container definitions, guards, and the devops-kb and dogfood demo workspaces used to validate the approach.

layers.name: Project Config & Tooling
layers.description: Root project anchors and tooling — package/tsconfig/MCP/Claude settings, ignore files, container ignore, the README/CLAUDE guides, the self-brain seed script, and the committed .vfkb brain store.

tour.title: Project Overview & Vision
tour.description: Start here to learn what vfkb is: a per-project knowledge substrate that gives AI agents durable memory through ONE engine exposed via three harness faces over an append-only JSONL brain. The README lays out the layout, build/test workflow, and the L4 scenario harness, while the DESIGN doc explains the architectural philosophy. Together they give you the map for the rest of the tour: storage kernel, engine facade, faces, the Track-1 continuity loop, guardrails, and evaluation.

tour.title: The Storage Kernel
tour.description: Everything in vfkb sits on top of this append-only JSONL store, the single source of truth. storage.ts resolves the brain directory, appends and materializes records (tombstone-aware), and maintains the context spine with content hashing; index-store.ts builds an in-memory lexical index over those materialized records with freshness invalidation; git.ts auto-commits the brain so memory survives across clones. Understanding this layer first is essential because every higher layer reads and writes through it.
tour.languageLesson: Append-only logs never mutate past records — updates and deletes are expressed as new records and tombstones, so the full history is always recoverable and writes are conflict-free.

tour.title: The Knowledge Envelope & Types
tour.description: Building on the raw JSONL records from Step 2, types.ts defines the shared vocabulary of the whole system: the Entry record contract plus entry types, zones, roles, and the provenance/validity shapes that encode derived trust. These types are the contract every layer agrees on, so the engine, reader, curator, distiller, and all three faces speak the same language. Learn this envelope before the engine, because the engine's logic is built entirely around manipulating these shapes.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript's discriminated unions let a single Entry type branch on a literal 'type' field, so the compiler narrows each entry to its correct shape and the engine can switch behavior safely per entry kind.

tour.title: The Engine Facade
tour.description: engine.ts is the hub of the system and the highest fan-out node in the graph — CLI, MCP server, curator, distiller, and reader all ground against it. It owns every write path (add, update, supersede, transition, capture), derives the decision/constitution family, computes injectability and reranking, and renders all context/resume output. This is the heart of the architecture: once you understand the facade, the surrounding modules become thin adapters around it.
tour.languageLesson: A facade pattern hides a subsystem's complexity behind one coherent interface — here a single module concentrates the write paths and rendering logic so callers never touch storage, indexing, or trust derivation directly.

tour.title: The Read & Search Path
tour.description: read.ts is the query side of the engine: it filters and reranks indexed entries by type, zone, role, tags, and status, applies a term-ratio floor to suppress weak lexical matches, and enforces the 'verified' trust filter so agents recall only corroborated knowledge. It exposes query and queryExplained, the latter surfacing why each entry was selected. This closes the loop from Step 2's index into the answers the faces hand back to agents.

tour.title: The Three Harness Faces
tour.description: With the engine and reader in place, vfkb exposes them through three thin faces over the same core. cli.ts is the Claude Code face — a command dispatcher plus the session-start, pre-tool-use, and post-tool-use hooks; mcp-server.ts exposes the nine kb_* tools over the Model Context Protocol SDK; pi-extension.ts wires the engine into Pi's in-process lifecycle hooks. The key lesson: one engine, three adapters — all three faces delegate to the same logic from Steps 4-5.

tour.title: Session Continuity & Resume
tour.description: session.ts is the first Track-1 addition: a per-session state model tracking injected and captured entry ids, signals, turn counts, and resume notes, persisted as JSON session records under the brain. It is what lets a face hand off context from one working session to the next via the resume flow exposed in Step 6's faces. This turns the static brain into a continuous memory across an agent's sessions.

tour.title: Auto-Distill & ACE Curator
tour.description: These tightly coupled modules form the self-improving loop on top of the engine. distiller.ts clusters captured error tool-calls by a normalized error signature and, on repeated corroboration, distills reusable 'gotcha' entries; curator.ts (the ACE curator) promotes corroborated entries to verified trust, archives or supersedes decisions, and merges lexical duplicates; counters.ts is the append-only signal ledger both rely on to tally corroboration. Together they let the brain learn from experience rather than only storing what it is told.

tour.title: Guardrails
tour.description: Two focused guardrails protect the integrity of the brain. secrets.ts scans incoming text for credential patterns and throws before any secret can be written, keeping sensitive data out of the store; gating.ts detects when a tool call would write into the brain directory and blocks it, preventing an agent from corrupting the append-only JSONL from inside a session. These deterministic checks are the safety net around every write path from Steps 4 and 6.

tour.title: Zero-Dependency Build
tour.description: package.json captures a defining architectural constraint: the engine itself is stdlib, with only the MCP SDK and zod as runtime deps, and tsconfig.json drives the plain tsc build into dist/. This minimal-surface choice is why vfkb ships as a lightweight, auditable substrate. Reviewing the manifest ties together the modules you have seen — the scripts here are exactly the build, test, CLI, and MCP entry points from Step 6.

tour.title: L4 Evaluation Harness
tour.description: The capstone: how vfkb proves it actually helps an agent. scenarios/l4-purpose.mjs is the dockerized, reproducible, dual-harness purpose-demonstration runner that scores whether the brain measurably improves agent behavior, and ADR-0022 records the evaluation methodology (N=3 trials, two-of-three demonstrated). This is the Definition of Done for agent-observable features — the live, metered top of the testing pyramid that backstops every layer you toured.
tour.languageLesson: Reproducible evaluation runs in containers pin the environment so results are comparable across runs; requiring a majority of N trials (here 2 of 3) guards against the inherent variance of LLM-driven behavior.
